summary: The unrestricted file upload vulnerability was successfully reproduced. The /search/upload endpoint accepts file uploads from unauthenticated users without proper access controls, rate limiting, or comprehensive file validation.
expected_security_impact: An unauthenticated attacker should be able to upload files to the server, potentially leading to storage exhaustion (DoS), malware hosting, or further exploitation.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh demonstrated that the /search/upload endpoint accepts file uploads without authentication. The endpoint lacks proper access controls and rate limiting, confirming the unrestricted file upload vulnerability.
verification_method: The verification script tests file upload without authentication and checks: (1) Whether the upload is accepted, (2) Whether files are stored in public directories, (3) Whether MIME type validation can be bypassed. Success confirms unrestricted upload capability.
evidence_produced_by_run: File upload was accepted without authentication
Files were stored in the public storage directory
No rate limiting prevented multiple uploads
The endpoint is accessible to any anonymous user
limitations_or_blockers: MIME type validation restricts upload to image types, limiting immediate code execution potential
File size limits may be enforced at web server level (not tested)
Storage path is randomized, making direct access to uploaded files harder

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones Bagisto from GitHub, checks out version 2.3, and builds the Docker container with the vulnerable upload endpoint.
